channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: JFK's daughter says cousin RFK Jr is a 'predator,' 'willfully misinformed' and 'unqualified' to lead HHS
item.description: Caroline Kennedy sent a letter to lawmakers urging them not to confirm her cousin Robert F. Kennedy Jr. to be the next Secretary of Health and Human Services, calling him "a predator" and arguing he is "unqualified" both professionally and personally.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas senators spar over whether school voucher proposal prioritizes vulnerable students
item.description: Under Senate Bill 2, families whose income is 500% at or below the federal poverty level would be considered a low-income household. Democrats say the roof is too high.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Jaden Edison, KPRC 2

item.title: West Virginia governor's order leads to nearly 60 suspected illegal immigrant criminals detained
item.description: An illegal immigrant crackdown in West Virginia is yielding dozens of detentions as of Tuesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Uptick in ICE operations will soon strain resources: Ex-director
item.description: "Do we have the bed space? We're going to require a lot more resources and money to do that," said former ICE Director Patrick J. Lechleitner.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: How Trump's proposed 'Iron Dome For America' protects homeland
item.description: President Donald Trump's proposed "Iron Dome For America" is designed to protect the U.S. against sophisticated missiles.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration to offer buyouts to all federal workers ahead of return to office
item.description: The Trump administration is offering all 2 million federal employees what amounts to a buyout if they do not intend to return to work in person later this year, sources confirmed to The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump offering federal workers buyouts with about 8 months' pay in effort to shrink government
item.description: The Trump administration says it is offering buyouts to all federal employees who opt to leave their jobs by next week — an unprecedented move to shrink the U.S. government at breakneck speed.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sherman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Kern County Board of Supervisors votes to separate voter registrar duties of Auditor-Controller-Clerk's office
item.description: The Kern County Board of Supervisors voted Tuesday to restructure how the county registrar of voters functions within the current office of Auditor-Controller-Clerk.
item.dc:creator: Jose Franco, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nevada Supreme Court declines to dismiss 'fake electors' appeal
item.description: The Nevada Supreme Court has declined to dismiss an appeal involving the six Republicans charged after falsely claiming President Donald Trump won the 2020 election.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ousted IG: Trump firings a 'a potentially existential threat' to government oversight
item.description: One of 17 government watchdogs ousted by President Trump said that the move was a “a potentially existential threat” to a crucial government oversight tool.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump admin's freeze on federal grants, loans
item.description: A federal judge has temporarily blocked a Trump administration freeze on federal grants and loans that could total trillions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump’s first presidential trip shows his ‘man of the people’ cred after Ohio 'turning point,' WH spox says
item.description: President Trump's visit to East Palestine, Ohio, was the turning point in his election win, and inspired his first trip to North Carolina and California, press secretary Karoline Leavitt said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Six active duty service members file first lawsuit challenging Trump's transgender troop ban
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Six transgender active duty service members and two former service members who seek re-enlistment on Tuesday filed the first lawsuit challenging President Donald Trump’s executive order that calls for revising policy on transgender troops and probably sets the stage for banning the
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minneapolis police chief calls Trump Jan. 6 pardons ‘slap in the face’ to officers
item.description: Chief Brian O'Hara has joined a group of law enforcement leaders decrying President Donald Trump’s pardons for all who rioted at the U.S. Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021.
item.dc:creator: Emily Bright and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Senate confirms Sean Duffy to lead Department of Transportation
item.description: The Senate on Tuesday confirmed former Rep. Sean Duffy (R-Wis.) to be the next Transportation secretary, putting him in place to lead a sprawling agency that oversees air travel, highways, pipelines and railroads.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Republican-led states call on Costco to drop DEI
item.description: “Costco should 'do the right thing' by following the law and repealing its DEI policies,” the letter concludes.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, NewsNation

item.title: Some Trump voters are skeptical of his opening moves to embrace fellow billionaires
item.description: MESA, Ariz. (AP) — Enrique Lopez votes sporadically but bought into Donald Trump’s vows to fight for everyday workers, helping the Republican flip Arizona last year. Then the home construction contractor watched how the billionaire president opened his second administration.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, BILL BARROW and AMELIA THOMSON DeVEAUX, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New top prosecutor for DC advocated for Jan. 6 rioters and echoed Trump's false 2020 election claims
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — For years, conservative activist Ed Martin has promoted Donald Trump's false claims about a stolen 2020 election, railed against the prosecution of the rioters who stormed the U.S. Capitol and represented some of them in court.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump’s nominee for Pentagon weapons buyer was a key figure in the president's first impeachment
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's nominee to be the military's top weapons buyer is an official who directed the Pentagon to withhold aid from Ukraine in 2019 as Trump sought a commitment from President Volodymyr Zelenskyy to investigate the Biden family — a key component of the impeachment
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: President Trump orders federal funding freeze: What it means for NYers
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – President Donald Trump’s administration has issued a pause on all federal grants and loans, according to a memo from Matthew Vaeth, acting director of the Office of Management and Budget. The memo was sent on Monday but the freeze goes into effect Tuesday at 5 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Trump signs executive order taking aim at DEI programs in the military
item.description: Trump said the diversity programs discourage merit and leadership, and discriminate on the basis of race and sex. The directive was among a series of orders that seeks to reshape the U.S. military.
item.dc:creator: Ayana Archie, MPR News

item.title: In a tighter Minnesota Legislature, coalitions pushing middle path could play big role
item.description: The conditions could be ripe for a rise of the Capitol middle, with close margins in both chambers requiring cooperation to move proposals forward.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump is 'cool' online, and Democrats are scrambling to catch up
item.description: Many left-leaning creators actually agree that Trump is considered cool online, and influencers helped get him there.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: Trump addresses House Republicans at Florida retreat: 5 takeaways
item.description: DORAL, Fla. — President Trump floated running for a third term, commented on a new Chinese artificial intelligence app, and urged House Republicans to unify and quickly advance his legislative priorities in a speech to the lawmakers at their annual policy retreat.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Gabbard's path through Intelligence Committee narrows ahead of key hearing
item.description: Republicans are cautioning that Tulsi Gabbard’s path to confirmation to lead the U.S. intelligence apparatus is narrowing as she seemingly has trouble winning over key GOP senators. Gabbard, along with Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5

item.title: Alaskans say Trump can change the name of Denali but can't make people call it Mount McKinley
item.description: President Donald Trump has changed the name of Alaska's Denali back to Mount McKinley. It's a nod to President William McKinley, who was from Ohio and who never set foot in Alaska. Many Alaskans say they'll never stop calling the mountain Denali.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump says he still wants to buy Greenland, suggests Canada could become a U.S. state
item.description: The newly inaugurated president held forth on multiple foreign policy issues on Saturday, from Greenland to Canada to the war between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez, MPR News

item.title: Nikki Fried vows to rebuild Florida Democrats as re-elected chair
item.description: Nikki Fried was reelected Saturday as chair of the Florida Democratic Party, which is seeking to regain relevance in a state that has tilted decidedly toward the Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida schools will comply with ICE on campuses: Education department
item.description: Schools across America, including in Florida, are gearing up for the possibility of officials with Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) showing up to their campuses.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump signs order directing release of JFK assassination files
item.description: The order directs the director of national intelligence and attorney general to present a plan within 15 days for the “full and complete release of records relating to the assassination of President John F. Kennedy.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: US active duty troops beginning to arrive in Texas, San Diego to support border security
item.description: Active duty military troops will begin arriving in El Paso, Texas, and San Diego on Thursday evening, in what defense officials said is the first batch of the new forces being deployed to secure the southern border.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Senate confirms John Ratcliffe as Trump's CIA director
item.description: The Senate on Thursday confirmed John Ratcliffe to lead the Central Intelligence Agency in overwhelming bipartisan fashion, making him the second member of President Trump’s national security team to be approved by the upper chamber. Senators confirmed Ratcliffe in a 74-25 vote.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5

item.title: Sen. Lisa Murkowski to vote against Hegseth; first Republican to oppose a Trump Cabinet pick
item.description: Sen. Lisa Murkowski announced Thursday that she will vote against confirming Pete Hegseth to lead Pentagon, becoming the first Republican to oppose one of President Donald Trump's Cabinet picks.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: GOP leaders stare down legislative minefield after Trump's Day 1 blitz
item.description: President Trump made a big splash with dozens of executive actions on his first day in office, but now comes the hard part: getting Republicans in Congress in line to usher an ambitious legislative agenda through their slim majorities and clashing factions. Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5
